name: Town of Chester v. Laroe Estates, Inc.
question: According to Article III of the U.S. Constitution, must intervenors to a lawsuit establish standing on their own, or may they join so long as there is a valid case or controversy between the named parties?
description: A case in which the Court determined that an intervenor to a lawsuit must meet the requirements of Article III standing if the intervenor wishes to pursue relief that was not already requested by the plaintiff.
